title: Bruins fans will need to wait a little longer for James Hagens’s NHL debut
author: Conor Ryan
bullets: James Hagens will not debut for the Bruins against the Tampa Bay Lightning on Saturday, per Coach Marco Sturm.
Boston can secure a playoff spot with any kind of win over Tampa, marking a return after a one-year absence.
Coach Marco Sturm emphasizes patience with Hagens, focusing on his adjustment to the team's system before playing.

title: Boston billionaires, historic figures land on Forbes’ ‘self-made’ lists
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: Noubar Afeyan, Herb Chambers, and Bill Cummings ranked on Forbes' 'The Greatest Self-Made Americans' list.
Forbes’ rankings consider financial success, obstacles overcome, and long-term impact with a 'Self-Made Score'.
Boston historical figures like Benjamin Franklin and Louisa May Alcott featured on the 'Greatest Historic Self-Made Americans' list.

title: What you need to know about the 2026 Boston Marathon
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: The 130th Boston Marathon on April 20, 2026, will feature 30,000 runners from over 130 countries.
Organized with six start waves, the marathon will include top athletes like John Korir and Sharon Lokedi.
Live coverage begins at 4 a.m. on WCVB Channel 5 and is available on the Very Local app.

title: Patriots reportedly adding former Titans assistant Shane Bowen to coaching staff
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The Patriots are adding Shane Bowen as an analyst, previously a defensive coordinator for the Giants and Titans.
Bowen worked with Patriots head coach Mike Vrabel at Ohio State, the Texans, and the Titans.
Bowen's hiring is part of a reshaping of New England’s defensive staff amid other recent changes.

title: Where are the public restrooms in Boston?
author: Annie Jonas
bullets: Boston offers public restrooms in places like libraries, firehouses, and some restaurants, accessible by an interactive map.
The Age Strong Commission created a map to locate Boston's public restrooms, emphasizing public health and accessibility.
Massachusetts law allows people with certain medical conditions to access employee-only restrooms in certain retail locations.

title: Masconomet school officials did not adequately respond to harassment claims, student athlete says
author: Ross Cristantiello
bullets: Charlotte Rollins claims Masconomet school officials inadequately addressed harassment and threats, including a sexual violence threat.
Two teachers stated school failed to discipline a coach and students after gender discrimination and retaliatory acts.
Superintendent Michael Harvey disputes allegations, citing privacy regulations, while School Committee Chair seeks legal perspective.

title: Report: Mass. drivers have paid more than $1 million extra at the pump since the Iran war began
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts drivers have spent over $137 million more on gas since the Iran War began, according to a report.
Families in Massachusetts face a 37.9% increase in home oil costs, adding $224 per tank, amid the Iran conflict.
Massachusetts Republicans propose suspending the state gas tax, while Governor Healey opposes it, despite similar actions in other states.

title: Mass. campgrounds still have availability for World Cup fans
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: Canoe River Campground in Mansfield has availability during World Cup dates, offering 300 sites within 7 miles of Boston Stadium.
Jellystone Park Cranberry Acres in Carver provides glamping cabins and RV sites, 40 miles from Boston Stadium, for World Cup fans.
Boston hotels are raising prices for World Cup games, and Massachusetts hosts seven matches at Gillette Stadium from June 13 to July 9.

title: This stroke survivor is running Boston for Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Tim Rafferty, a Boston man, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon to support Spaulding Rehabilitation Hospital.
In 2019, Rafferty suffered a hemorrhagic stroke leaving his left side paralyzed; he later rehabilitated at Spaulding.
Despite vowing never to run again after his first marathon in 2021, Rafferty is participating again to give back.

title: ‘I appreciated that’: Jayson Tatum credited Knicks fans’ applause in MSG return
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Jayson Tatum returned to Madison Square Garden, site of his injury, during the Celtics' 112-106 loss to the Knicks.
Tatum appreciated the applause from Knicks fans during introductions, calling it a significant moment in his comeback.
Despite pregame nerves, Tatum relished the atmosphere at MSG, noting it remains a memorable part of his career story.
